title: Design a Global Content Delivery Network
description: Learn how to cache content at the 'Edge'. Understand PoPs, Anycast routing, and cache hierarchies.

steps.detail: Drag an API Server onto the canvas. This is your 'Source of Truth'.

explanation.what: The server where your original web content (images, videos, HTML) is stored before being cached.
explanation.why: Even with a CDN, you need a central point to upload and manage your files. When a CDN node doesn't have a file, it fetches it from here.
explanation.tradeoff: Vulnerable to 'thundering herd' if the CDN cache is flushed simultaneously across the globe.
explanation.realWorld: Netflix uses AWS S3 as their origin for petabytes of video content before it gets pushed to their Open Connect CDN.
explanation.capacity: Origins only see ~1-5% of total traffic after the CDN is warm.

steps.detail: Drag a CDN node and connect it in front of your Origin.

explanation.what: A network of globally distributed proxy servers that cache content close to users.
explanation.why: Speed of light is a bottleneck. 100ms round-trip to a US origin is unacceptable for a user in London. A London CDN node serves them in <5ms.
explanation.tradeoff: Cost and complexity. Purging a global cache (invalidation) is notoriously difficult to get perfectly right in real-time.
explanation.realWorld: Cloudflare handles ~20% of the internet's HTTP traffic through their network of 300+ Point of Presence (PoP) locations.
explanation.capacity: Modern CDNs handle 100+ Tbps of global throughput.

steps.detail: Connect a Load Balancer between the CDN and the Origin.

explanation.what: A dedicated regional layer (Proxy) that sits between the edge nodes and the origin.
explanation.why: Origin Shielding prevents thousands of edge nodes from all hitting your origin at once for a missing file. They hit the shield first, which collapses multiple requests into one fetch.
explanation.tradeoff: Adds another layer of potential failure and slightly more latency for cache misses (origin fetches).
explanation.realWorld: Fastly's Origin Shielding often reduces origin traffic by 99% for viral content.
explanation.capacity: Origin shields focus on request consolidation, not just raw volume.

questions.question: What is 'Anycast' routing primarily used for in a CDN?

options.text: Broadcasting data to all servers simultaneously
Routing a user to the geographically closest server using the same IP

explanation.correct: Anycast allows multiple servers to share the same IP. BGP routing then sends the user's packets to the 'closest' node on the network, minimizing latency naturally.

wrongAnswers.c: Anycast only routes to one destination, even if many share the IP.
wrongAnswers.d: Anycast is a networking protocol; while it hides IPs as a side effect, its primary job is routing efficiency.
